objective: Compare fractions using multiple strategies: same denominator, same numerator, and benchmark fractions (0, 1/2, 1)

pretest.question: Which fraction is greater than 1/2: 2/8 or 5/6?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.4 'Compare Fractions' pp.365-368
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 벤치마크 1/2 — 빠른 비교
learn.content: You already know two strategies. Strategy 1: Same denominator → compare numerators. Strategy 2: Same numerator → smaller denominator wins. Now here's Strategy 3: USE BENCHMARKS. The benchmarks are 0, 1/2, and 1. Ask yourself: Is this fraction less than 1/2, equal to 1/2, or greater than 1/2? Example: Compare 1/8 and 5/6. 1/8 is less than 1/2 (small piece). 5/6 is greater than 1/2 (almost a whole). So 1/8 < 5/6. 예: Is 3/4 greater than or less than 1/2? → Greater than 1/2. (Half of 4 is 2. Since 3 > 2, we have 3/4 > 2/4 = 1/2.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.4 'Compare Fractions' pp.365-368
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 1/2와의 거리 판정
learn.content: How to tell if a fraction is greater than, less than, or equal to 1/2: Look at the numerator and denominator. Half the denominator = the numerator for 1/2. Example: denominator is 8 → half of 8 is 4 → so 4/8 = 1/2. If numerator > half the denominator → greater than 1/2. If numerator < half the denominator → less than 1/2. 예: Is 3/8 greater than, less than, or equal to 1/2? → Less than 1/2. (Half of 8 is 4. Numerator 3 < 4, so 3/8 < 1/2.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.4 'Compare Fractions' pp.365-368
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 전략 선택 — 어떤 방법?
learn.content: Choosing a strategy: Step 1 — Check: same denominator? Use Strategy 1. Step 2 — Check: same numerator? Use Strategy 2. Step 3 — Neither? Use the 1/2 benchmark (Strategy 3). If both fractions are on the same side of 1/2, you may need a model or number line to decide. 예: Compare 2/6 and 5/8. Which strategy would you use first? → Benchmark strategy (Strategy 3). (Different numerators, different denominators → use benchmarks. 2/6 < 1/2 (because 2 < 3). 5/8 > 1/2 (because 5 > 4). So 2/6 < 5/8.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.4 'Compare Fractions' pp.365-368
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 벤치마크 1 — 1에 가까운 쪽이 큼
learn.content: 1과의 거리로 비교: 1에 가까운 쪽이 큰 분수.   거리: 1 − 분수 = (b − a)/b.   예: 5/6 vs 7/8 → 1−5/6=1/6, 1−7/8=1/8 → 1/8이 작음 → 7/8이 1에 더 가까움 → 7/8 > 5/6. 🔍 같은 분자에 가까운 분자 분수 비교에 효과적.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.4 'Compare Fractions' pp.365-368
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 남은 조각 전략 — 1까지 얼마?
learn.content: 분수가 1에 얼마나 가까운지: '남은 조각'으로 판단.   3/4 → 1/4 남음.   5/8 → 3/8 남음. 남은 조각이 작은 쪽이 1에 가까움. 예: 3/4 (남은 1/4) vs 5/8 (남은 3/8) → 1/4 < 3/8 → 3/4 더 가까움 → 3/4 > 5/8.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.4 'Compare Fractions' pp.365-368
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 일반 비교 3단계 루틴
learn.content: 어떤 분수든 비교 3단계:   단계 1) 같은 전체인지 확인.   단계 2) 같은 분모/분자가 있나? → 직접 규칙.   단계 3) 둘 다 다르면 → 벤치마크 (1/2 또는 1) 사용. 예: 1/8 vs 5/6 → 1) 같은 전체 ✓, 2) 모두 다름, 3) 1/8 < 1/2 < 5/6 → 1/8 < 5/6.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.4 'Compare Fractions' pp.365-368
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 결정 트리 — 빠른 길 선택
learn.content: 분수 비교 결정 트리:   Q1) 분모 같음? → 분자 큰 쪽 (L2 규칙).   Q2) 분자 같음? → 분모 작은 쪽 (L3 규칙).   Q3) 둘 다 다름? → 벤치마크 1/2 (한쪽 < 1/2 < 다른쪽).   Q4) 둘 다 1/2의 같은 쪽? → 1까지 거리 비교. 예: 2/3 vs 5/6 → 모두 > 1/2 → 1까지: 1/3 vs 1/6 → 1/6 작음 → 5/6 더 큼.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.4 'Compare Fractions' pp.365-368
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 실생활 — 같은 크기 가정
learn.content: 분수 비교는 항상 '같은 전체' 가정 위에서.   같은 크기 피자, 같은 길이 막대, 같은 양 물.   다른 크기 → 분수만으로 비교 불가. 예: 큰 피자 1/4 vs 작은 피자 1/2 → 분수만 비교하면 1/2 > 1/4지만, 실제 양은 큰 피자 1/4가 더 많을 수 있음. 🔍 문제 풀 때 항상 '같은 ___' 표현 확인.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.4 'Compare Fractions' pp.365-368
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: Compare: 1/6 ○ 7/8
try.hint: Use benchmarks. Is 1/6 more or less than 1/2? What about 7/8?
guided_steps: Step 1: Different denominators, different numerators → use benchmarks.
Step 2: 1/6 — half of 6 is 3, numerator 1 < 3 → less than 1/2.
Step 3: 7/8 — half of 8 is 4, numerator 7 > 4 → greater than 1/2.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.4 'Compare Fractions' pp.365-368
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: Two same-size sandwiches. Amy ate 5/6 of hers. Ben ate 3/8 of his. Who ate more? Explain your strategy.
try.answer: Amy; 5/6 > 3/8. 5/6 > 1/2 and 3/8 < 1/2, so Amy ate more.
guided_steps: Step 1: 5/6 → half of 6 is 3, 5 > 3 → greater than 1/2.
Step 2: 3/8 → half of 8 is 4, 3 < 4 → less than 1/2.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.4 'Compare Fractions' pp.365-368
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: Ryan says you can only compare fractions if they have the same denominator. Is he right? Explain and give an example.
practice_r3.answer: Ryan is NOT right. You can also compare fractions with the same numerator (compare piece sizes) or use benchmark fractions like 1/2. Example: 1/8 < 5/6 because 1/8 < 1/2 and 5/6 > 1/2.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.4 'Compare Fractions' pp.365-368

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: Explain how you know that 2/3 > 3/8 WITHOUT using a picture. Name the strategy.
practice_r3.answer: Benchmark strategy. 2/3 > 1/2 (because 2 > half of 3 = 1.5). 3/8 < 1/2 (because 3 < half of 8 = 4). A fraction greater than 1/2 is always greater than a fraction less than 1/2. So 2/3 > 3/8.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.4 'Compare Fractions' pp.365-368
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
